title: Evaluate remaining typelift packages (Swiftx / Basis / Concurrent / TypeLift / Forge / Slip) for substrate-action
description: Phase C.4 (PRIORITY 4) catch-all for typelift packages not prioritized as immediate forks: Swiftx (functional Swift: functors/monads/applicatives — likely mostly subsumed by modern Swift stdlib), Basis (Haskell-style Either/Maybe — also mostly subsumed by Result/Optional), Concurrent (pre-Swift-Concurrency primitives — DEFINITELY obsoleted by Apple's structured concurrency), TypeLift (the org's flagship — type-level programming utilities; needs direct read to assess), Forge (type-level operations — needs direct read), Slip (possibly parser combinators — substrate has hand-rolled parsing in places). For each, the audit (Phase B) recommends one of: fork-modernize (graduate to its own bead), adopt-pattern-fresh (write substrate-flavored equivalent without forking), archive-as-reference (keep in maintainers/ but no fork), skip (uninteresting). This bead closes when every remaining package has a recorded disposition. Likely outcome: Concurrent → skip, Swiftx/Basis → archive-as-reference, TypeLift/Forge → fork-if-genuinely-useful (graduate to new beads), Slip → adopt-pattern-fresh-or-skip.
